sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 18, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Two 3×3 green tokens with colored centers (white=active, charcoal=inactive). Arrow keys move the active token in 3-cell steps. MOUSE on a center swaps activation. Purple wall blocks movement. Two containers on left and right platforms. Goal: place tokens into containers.
- Plan: Move the active right token UP over the purple wall, RIGHT past it, then DOWN into the right container.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 17 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the board clearly. Looking at the ascii:
```
Row 30: wNNNWNNwwwwwwpp
Row 31: wNcNWWNwwwwwwpp
Row 32: wNNNWNNwwwwwwpp
```
The left token is at cols 21-23 (N N N / N c N / N N N) - a 3x3 green token with charcoal center.
The right token is at cols 25-26 (N N / W N / N N) - only 3x2 now, with white center.
Between them at col 24 is W (white).
So the right token has been compressed from 3x3 to 3x2. It seems like when I moved it LEFT, it collided with the left token and got squished. The token lost its leftmost column and the center changed from white to... wait, the center is still W (white) at [31,25].
Actually wait, looking at row 31: `wNcNWWNwwwwwwpp`
- Col 21: N (left token left edge)
- Col 22: c (left token center)
- Col 23: N (left token right edge)
- Col 24: W (white - bridge?)
- Col 25: W (white - right token center?)
- Col 26: N (right token right edge)
Hmm, the right token's center is at col 25, which is W. The token's green parts are at cols 25-26 (N at col 26) and... wait, the token is supposed to be N (green) with a center. Let me re-read:
- Row 30: ...WNN... (cols 24-26: W, N, N)
- Row 31: ...WWN... (cols 24-26: W, W, N)
- Row 32: ...WNN... (cols 24-26: W, N, N)
So the right token is:
- Col 24: W (white) - not green
- Col 25: N, W, N (green, white center, green)
- Col 26: N, N, N (green)
Wait, that doesn't match the segmentation. The segmentation says:
- id=7 color=W px=4 [30,24]-[32,25] - white object
- id=8 color=N px=5 [30,25]-[32,26] - green token
Let me re-examine. The ascii shows at cols 24-26:
Row 30: W N N
Row 31: W W N
Row 32: W N N
So the white object (id=7) is at [30,24]-[32,25], which includes:
- [30,24]: W
- [31,24]: W
- [30,25]: N (but this is part of the green token!)
- [31,25]: W
- [32,25]: N (but this is part of the green token!)
Wait, that doesn't make sense. The segmentation says id=7 is color W with 4 pixels. Let me count the W cells in that region:
- [30,24]: W
- [31,24]: W
- [31,25]: W
That's only 3 W cells, not 4. Unless I'm miscounting.
Actually, I think the issue is that the token got damaged or merged with the bridge. This is getting complicated. Let me just try to figure out what the game wants me to do.
Looking at the board, I see:
1. Left platform with a container (dark square) and a green token with charcoal center
2. Right platform with a container (dark square) and no token
3. Purple wall between platforms
4. Gap bridge connecting left platform to the area before the wall
5. A damaged/squished green token on the bridge
The goal seems to be to put tokens into containers. The left token needs to go into the left container (which is below and to the left of the token). The right token was supposed to go to the right container but got stuck.
Wait, I think I need to reconsider the game mechanics. Maybe the tokens aren't supposed to collide with each other. Maybe I should have moved the left token first, out of the way, before moving the right token.
Let me try a different approach:
1. Activate the left token (click its center)
2. Move the left token into the left container (DOWN and LEFT)
3. Then deal with the right token
But first, let me check if the right token is still movable. It's at [30,25]-[32,26] with a white center. Let me try RIGHT to see if it can move back.
Actually, I realize I've been wasting a lot of actions. Let me think about this more carefully.
The key question is: what's the goal? I think it's to place tokens into containers. The left container is at [32,11]-[36,15] and the right container is at [26,44]-[30,48].
For the left token to reach the left container:
- Current: [30,21]-[32,23]
- Target: [32,11]-[36,15] (specifically the interior [33,12]-[35,14])
- Need: LEFT and DOWN
For the right token to reach the right container:
- Current: [30,25]-[32,26] (damaged)
- Target: [26,44]-[30,48] (specifically the interior [27,45]-[29,47])
- Need: UP and RIGHT
The right token can't move RIGHT (blocked by purple wall) and can't move UP (no platform). So maybe the right token needs to go a different route, or maybe I need to first clear the left token out of the way.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the board. The right token is currently on the gap bridge. If I move it LEFT, it collides with the left token. If I move it RIGHT, it hits the purple wall. If I move it UP or DOWN, there's no platform.
I think the game might be broken now because I squished the token. Or maybe the game expects me to do something specific.
Let me try clicking on the left token's center to activate it, then move it into the container.
Actually, let me first try RIGHT on the current active token to see if it can move back to its original position.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model revised: The right token got squished when moving LEFT into the left token's space. It's now a 3x2 token on the bridge. UP/DOWN blocked (no platform), RIGHT blocked (purple wall), LEFT blocked (left token). Need to activate the left token and move it first.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First, let me try RIGHT to see if the active token can move back
# If not, I'll activate the left token
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
